---
title: "What is a forecast, and is it any good?"
site: distill::distill_website
comments: false
about:
  template: jolla
  links:
    - icon: chat-fill
      text: Start the workshop
      href: https://nfidd.github.io/iddconf2026/sessions/introduction-and-course-overview.html
    - icon: calendar-event
      text: Timetable
      href: https://nfidd.github.io/iddconf2026/timetable.html
    - icon: github
      text: Visit us on GitHub
      href: https://github.com/nfidd/iddconf2026
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

A half-day workshop for [IDDconf 2026](https://iddconf.com), run on the morning of Tuesday 7 July 2026 by Kath Sherratt and Sam Abbott.

We explore how to visualise and interpret infectious disease forecasts, evaluate forecast performance and identify limitations, and combine multiple forecasts into ensembles.
We introduce each concept and then work through examples implemented in R.

This workshop is a short, forecasting-focused selection from the fuller course on [Nowcasting and forecasting infectious disease dynamics](https://nfidd.github.io/nfidd/).
Please see the [timetable](timetable.qmd) for the plan for the morning and [getting started](getting-started.qmd) to set up.

All materials here are provided under the permissive [MIT License](https://github.com/nfidd/iddconf2026/blob/main/LICENSE).
